name: dobachi-skills
description: Personal skills marketplace for Claude Code

plugins.name: fact-checker
plugins.description: Automated fact-checking for AI-generated documents. Extracts claims, verifies via web search and headless browser (Puppeteer), and generates a Markdown report.

plugins.name: verify-content
plugins.description: Integrated content verification skill. Extracts claims, verifies against external sources, and compiles references in a single workflow.

plugins.name: build
plugins.description: Detects and executes the appropriate build command for your project. Supports Node.js, Rust, Python, Go, Makefile, and other major project types.

plugins.name: checkpoint
plugins.description: Checkpoint management for the AI instruction system. Tracks task start, progress, and completion, and manages instruction usage state.

plugins.name: commit-and-report
plugins.description: Commits changes, pushes to remote, and reports progress to a GitHub Issue in a single workflow.

plugins.name: commit-safe
plugins.description: Assists with safe, selective commits: reviews changes before committing, suggests file-specific commits for large changesets, and prevents blanket 'git add -A'. Bundles a self-contained commit helper that blocks AI-signed commit messages.

plugins.name: github-issues
plugins.description: Fetches GitHub Issues, aggregates by label, analyzes priorities, and organizes tasks with actionable suggestions.

plugins.name: reload-instructions
plugins.description: Updates the AI instruction submodule to the latest version and reloads ROOT_INSTRUCTION.

plugins.name: reload-and-reset
plugins.description: Updates the AI instruction system to the latest version and resets AI behavior to align with the instructions.

plugins.name: evidence-check
plugins.description: Verifies references and citations in reports and papers, performing evidence-based fact-checking.

plugins.name: web-api-dev
plugins.description: Expert assistant for designing and implementing production-ready RESTful Web APIs.

plugins.name: cli-tool-dev
plugins.description: CLI development expert that helps design and implement user-friendly command-line tools.

plugins.name: data-analyst
plugins.description: Comprehensive data analysis expert covering statistical insights, visualization, and machine learning.

plugins.name: technical-writer
plugins.description: Expert assistant for creating high-quality technical documents including API docs, user guides, and technical blog posts.

plugins.name: academic-researcher
plugins.description: Comprehensive academic research expert covering paper writing, literature review, citation management, and research methodology.

plugins.name: business-consultant
plugins.description: Comprehensive business consulting using McKinsey/BCG methodologies, from strategy planning to execution support.

plugins.name: project-manager
plugins.description: Project manager skill for comprehensive planning, execution, monitoring, and completion of projects.

plugins.name: startup-advisor
plugins.description: Startup advisor providing practical advice and strategic guidance from launch through growth stages.

plugins.name: python-expert
plugins.description: Python development expert supporting clean code practices, performance optimization, and test-driven development.

plugins.name: code-reviewer
plugins.description: Provides constructive code reviews focusing on quality, security, performance, and maintainability.

plugins.name: marp-slides
plugins.description: Assists in creating effective, professional presentations using the Marp slide framework.

plugins.name: tauri-dev
plugins.description: Development expert for designing and building Tauri v2 desktop and mobile applications.

plugins.name: tauri-research
plugins.description: Research assistant for investigating Tauri-related questions via web search and official documentation.

plugins.name: competitive-analysis
plugins.description: Competitive analysis skill that researches competitor products via web search, compares features/pricing/differentiation, and generates comparison matrices.

plugins.name: business-model-canvas
plugins.description: Business model design skill that generates structured documents following Business Model Canvas and Lean Canvas frameworks.

plugins.name: market-sizing
plugins.description: Market sizing skill that estimates TAM/SAM/SOM using top-down and bottom-up approaches with evidence-backed reports.

plugins.name: strategy-memo
plugins.description: Strategy memo skill that structures business ideas into a 4-layer framework (Market/Value Proposition/Technology/Execution) and checks for logical gaps.

plugins.name: pptx-design
plugins.description: Expert guidance for designing professional PowerPoint presentations: typography, color, layout, data visualization, structural diagrams, deck genres, and accessibility.

plugins.name: pptx-build
plugins.description: Generate clean, white-based .pptx files that don't look AI-made — built on python-pptx. Default mode: grid-anchored from-scratch layout with no drifting decorative bands. Template-fill mode: open a real corporate .pptx/.potx and write into its layouts and placeholders, inheriting the template's master/theme/fonts/logos. LibreOffice preview.

plugins.name: faithful-translation
plugins.description: Faithful translation skill that produces source-faithful translations across any language pair, with a parallel sentence ledger for verification, terminology preservation, and translator's notes.

plugins.name: document-summary
plugins.description: Document and literature summarization skill with Executive and Professional modes, mandatory source-grounded Claim Ledger, and explicit Source vs Inference separation to prevent hallucinated content.

plugins.name: literature-search
plugins.description: Literature search, citation chasing, snowballing, author analysis, and BibTeX export using free official APIs (Semantic Scholar + OpenAlex + CrossRef). Honest Google Scholar alternative — no scraping, no paid services.

plugins.name: document-figures
plugins.description: Extract figures from existing documents (PDF / Word / PowerPoint / web) with provenance and create new structural diagrams (Mermaid-first), producing a Figure Ledger that chains into document-summary.

plugins.name: doc-coauthoring
plugins.description: Guided, knowledge-grounded co-authoring workflow for substantial documents (proposals, specs, decision docs, technical articles, whitepapers). Three optional, composable stages — context gathering, section-by-section drafting, and reader-testing — grounding facts in a domain knowledge base with cited sources and consistent terminology. Drafts into a working file, preserves human [人] edits, and hands off critical review to doc-review, restructuring to doc-refactor, fact-checking to verify-content/evidence-check, de-AI restyle to ai-tell-reducer/humanize-prose, and translation to faithful-translation. Bilingual JA/EN.

plugins.name: doc-refactor
plugins.description: Refactor prose documents — restructure and de-duplicate without changing meaning, the way code refactoring preserves behavior. Runs a diagnose-first workflow (reverse outline → issue inventory → confirm structural moves → refactor → change log) that preserves every claim, fact, figure, and the author's voice, and flags substantive problems instead of silently fixing them.

plugins.name: doc-review
plugins.description: Critical, read-only review of a prose document — the prose counterpart of /code-review. Hunts substantive weaknesses (unsound argument, buried conclusion, thin evidence, unstated assumptions, missing counterargument, audience mismatch, internal contradiction) across a genre-weighted rubric, adversarially verifies its own findings to kill sycophancy and nitpicks, and returns a prioritized findings report the author decides on — without editing the text. Hands off rewriting to doc-refactor, fact-checking to verify-content, restyling to ai-tell-reducer. Bilingual JA/EN.

plugins.name: math-paper
plugins.description: Mathematical paper writing support: notation consistency (Symbol Ledger), theorem/proof structure, equation cross-references, and journal/arXiv submission preparation. Works with LaTeX and Markdown+math; delegates citations to literature-search and fact-checking to fact-checker.

plugins.name: humanize-prose
plugins.description: Rewrite AI-generated or AI-assisted text to read as human-authored. Reduces common AI tells (em-dash overuse, 'delve'/'leverage', tricolons, reflex bullet lists, 'いかがでしたでしょうか', generic openers/closers) while preserving meaning, register, and facts. Bilingual (English / Japanese), reader-experience focused — not detector evasion.

plugins.name: ai-tell-reducer
plugins.description: Reduce the "AI-ness" of writing — uniform sentence rhythm, reflexive hedging, vague abstraction, formulaic scaffolding, inflated vocabulary, and surface tics (em-dash / bold / rule-of-three overuse) — while preserving meaning, facts, register, and the author's voice, and without fabricating anything. Works on Japanese and English text.

plugins.name: archimate-ea
plugins.description: Conversational ArchiMate 3.2 enterprise-architecture facilitator. Elicits requirements and designs the full layered model with the user into one YAML running model (single source of truth), validates it against the metamodel, then generate-forwards PlantUML views and Open Group Exchange XML for the Archi tool.

plugins.name: archimate-native
plugins.description: Brownfield ArchiMate: work conversationally on an EXISTING .archimate file (born in Archi) as the source of truth — read/understand, review (metamodel + quality), interpret, and surgically edit in place (round-trip-safe; ids/geometry/folders/styles preserved). Reuses the canonical ArchiMate 3.2 metamodel via a version-aware native vocabulary bridge; emits Markdown reports and PlantUML. Counterpart to archimate-ea (greenfield).

plugins.name: tech-selector
plugins.description: Select concrete middleware/runtime from an ArchiMate EA model's non-functional requirements: weighted, sensitivity-checked evaluation, an ADR for the rationale, and write-back of the decision into the model's Technology-layer elements. Bridges archimate-ea (design) to archimate-to-impl (implementation).

plugins.name: archimate-to-impl
plugins.description: Bridge an ArchiMate EA model's Application layer to implementation: derive a requirement→component→service→api→task traceability matrix, OpenAPI skeletons per ApplicationService, DataObject entity stubs, and a per-component task list, then check for orphans (unimplemented requirements, untraced components). Hands structured input to the dev skills.

plugins.name: ea-delivery
plugins.description: Thin conductor for end-to-end EA-driven delivery: sequences requirements → design → middleware selection → implementation → service across the specialist skills (archimate-ea, tech-selector, archimate-to-impl, dev skills), carries ea-model.yaml as the through-line, gates each handoff, and tracks progress and back-edges in a delivery ledger. Delegates all phase work.

plugins.name: optimization-modeling
plugins.description: Mathematical optimization / decision-engineering expert: formulate decisions as LP/MILP/convex/stochastic models, pick and drive a solver, handle uncertainty (stochastic & robust), and validate solutions with feasibility and sensitivity checks. For decision-support algorithms beyond descriptive statistics/ML.

plugins.name: plugin-platform
plugins.description: Design a plugin / extension platform (e.g. an algorithm marketplace): a stable versioned contract (SDK), a registry with discovery and compatibility, and safe execution of untrusted code via sandboxing, resource limits, and denial of ambient authority.

plugins.name: event-driven-service
plugins.description: Design event-driven, asynchronous, and real-time services beyond request/response REST: messaging (queues/streams), async workers, scheduled/round-based jobs, and WebSocket/SSE push, with correctness under concurrency — idempotency, ordering, delivery guarantees, and backpressure.

plugins.name: simulation-harness
plugins.description: Build simulation and backtesting harnesses to evaluate algorithms and system behavior: generate scenarios, run discrete-event or time-stepped models, replay history, collect metrics, and compare variants (A/B) reproducibly under fixed seeds. Feeds scenarios to optimization-modeling.

plugins.name: web-frontend-dev
plugins.description: Build production web front-ends (SPA and beyond): component architecture, state management, API data-fetching with caching, forms and data tables, auth/session UI, and accessibility. Turns frontend-design direction into a real implementation and consumes OpenAPI contracts.

plugins.name: agent-delegate
plugins.description: Delegate a task to another CLI coding agent — Codex (codex-delegate), Antigravity/agy (agy-delegate), or Claude Code (claude-code-delegate) — from within your current agent. Explicit-invocation only, read-first routing, and a preview→confirm→apply gate for any write. Bundles three skills.
